violations.text: Raw animal foods (such as whole intact beef) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as deli meats) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Food items were rearranged during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (deli slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Establishment is to clean and sanitize equipment prior to returning unit to service. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

Washed salad greens were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Date marks were placed on food items during the time of inspeciton. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

Standing water was observed on the cookline prep unit and the floor of the cookline. Per PIC, water originated from an active roof leak that was discovered this morning. All food items had been relocated prior to time of inspection. Per PIC, school is working with contractor to remediate roof leakage. All areas where standing water from leakage had come in contact with were cleaned and sanitized during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Employee placed protective hair restraint on their head during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

Wood is used in the construction of floor in the cookline area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. Per PIC, a drainage backup was discovered prior to break in system beneath the floor and was remediated during that time. Per PIC, they are waiting on contractor to complete the refinishing of the floor where blockage was cut out. New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2026

The roof in the cookline area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Per PIC, the standing water observed in cookline area originated from leak in the roof of building. Establishment is to remediate leakage and clean and sanitize all areas where water has come in contact with. New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (deli slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Deli slicer was unassembled, cleaned, and sanitized during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/24/2025

Tuna filet was observed thawed in 4-door refrigerator #1 with intact reduced oxygen packaging (ROP). Seafood in ROP packaging shall be removed from packaging prior to thawing to allow the reintroduction of oxygen to food items. Tuna filets were observed at 34-35 degrees Fahrenheit and were removed from their original packaging during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/24/2025
The back-flow prevention device on the 1-bay prep sink was observed to be leaking when water was turned on. Plumbing systems shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. New Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2024

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2024

The cutting boards in the kitchen are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2024

The door jamb located in the walk-in cooler was not in good repair and observed to be separating from the cooler paneling. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/07/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following areas have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris; -Fry fridge -Grill Fridge drawers -Cook line drawers -Prep unit door gaskets -Walk-in cooler fan guards and door gaskets Non-food contact surfaces shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to prevent the accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, or other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2024
The drain for the prep sink was observed to draining slowly and overflowing onto the floor. plumbing including drains must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/07/2024
The receptacle in the women's toilet room is lacking a cover. A toilet room used by females shall be provided with a covered receptacle for sanitary napkins. New Violation. Correct By: 09/25/2024

One of the hood system lights was observed with stagnant water within the protective shielding. The source of the water is unknown. The kitchen is temporarily closed and is not being used for active food preparation and will reopen later in the school year for food service. Establishment is to provide evidence of resolution to inspector prior to commencement/continuation of food service. New Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2024

Food contact surfaces of cooking utensils and ice machine were observed with food debris and pink residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2024
Spray bottles used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2024

Doors to the outside, located at the side of the kitchen, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 03/21/2024

Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water. Ice scoops observed stored on top of the ice machine. Utensils must be stored in a clean, dry location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2024
Scoops observed resting in dry ingredients. Scoops must be stored with handles above food product to avoid contamination. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the commercial mixers have an accumulation of dried food debris. Non-food contact surfaces of commercial mixers must be cleaned properly. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2024
Prep sink is lacking a proper air gap. Air gap is needed to prevent back-up of contaminated water/sewage into sink. New Violation. Correct By: 03/21/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was (00ppm) parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Chlorine sanitizing solution was observed to be light in color. Sanitizer was swapped out with a new container. Machine was primed and a reading of 100ppm was achieved at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/27/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) was observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/27/2023

The non-food contacts surface of the dried ingredients bins and lower-level shelving on prep tables and Hood above Grill area were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 10/27/2023

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was (00) parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Establishment shall sanitize using 3 bay sink and Quat sanatizer until Mechanical Machine is reading proper ppm. Repair Service was contacted during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/14/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the celling and air vents throughout the kitchen were observed with an accumulation of dust. Establishment shall have the celling and air vents cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 06/14/2023
